projects.name: my-game
projects.description: Build me a Game Boy Advance-style Advance Wars turn-based strategy game

buildHistory.description: Build me a Game Boy Advance-style Advance Wars turn-based strategy game
buildHistory.prompt:
Build me a Game Boy Advance-style Advance Wars turn-based strategy game. I want:
Turn-based tactical combat with a grid-based map (like Advance Wars)
Multiple unit types: Infantry, Tanks, Artillery, Planes, Ships
GBA pixel art aesthetic - 16-bit style graphics, chunky pixels, retro color palette
Two-player mode - Red vs Blue armies taking turns
Unit movement and combat - Click to select unit, click destination to move, attack enemies in range
Victory conditions - Capture enemy HQ or destroy all units
Simple UI - Unit stats, turn indicator, action buttons
Tech stack: Use HTML5 Canvas with JavaScript/TypeScript for rendering. Make it playable in browser. Include:
Grid-based map system
Unit sprites (can be simple colored rectangles with pixel art style)
Turn management system
Combat calculation system
Pathfinding for unit movement
Save/load game state
Visual style: Game Boy Advance pixel art - 240x160 resolution feel, limited color palette, chunky sprites. Use a retro color scheme (greens, browns, blues like classic GBA games).
Make it fully playable with mouse/touch controls. Include a README with instructions.

projects.name: my-vue-app
projects.description: "Build me a Vue 3 single-page app called Agent Prime Flow: a full-screen interactive audio-reactive...

buildHistory.description: "Build me a Vue 3 single-page app called Agent Prime Flow: a full-screen interactive audio-reactive...
buildHistory.prompt:
"Build me a Vue 3 single-page app called Agent Prime Flow: a full-screen interactive audio-reactive particle visualization.
Use Three.js for the WebGL particle system (start with 10k-50k particles for performance, make it scalable).
Key features:

Full-screen canvas with beautiful fluid/particle flows in vibrant colors (purples, pinks, blues—like wavy noise fields or Refik Anadol vibes).
Microphone access via Web Audio API—analyze volume/frequencies in real-time.
Modes toggle: Breath (volume pushes particles like wind), Music (bass/mids/treble drive movement, color shifts, rotation), Fireworks/Snaps (detect loud peaks for burst effects).
Overlay controls panel: sliders for particle count, speed, force strength, color palette, trail length; mode selector; reset button.
Auto-request mic permission on load, with fallback message if denied.
Smooth animations, post-processing effects (bloom, glow) for that premium look.
Dark background, responsive for desktop/mobile.
Bonus: simple AI chat sidebar where I describe a 'vibe' (e.g., 'cosmic ocean waves with neon glow') and it suggests parameter tweaks or code snippets to hot-reload.

buildHistory.description:
Create a new Electron + React project called 'TestApp' with the following specifications:
Project St...
buildHistory.prompt:
Create a new Electron + React project called 'TestApp' with the following specifications:
Project Structure:
Main Electron process with TypeScript
React frontend with TypeScript
Webpack configuration for both main and renderer
Basic UI with a counter component
Dependencies:
Electron
React & React DOM
TypeScript
Webpack & webpack-cli
HTML Webpack Plugin
CSS & style loaders
Scripts:
npm run dev - concurrent development servers
npm run build - production build
npm start - run the built app
Batch Files:
Create Windows batch files (.bat) for easy execution
Ensure Node.js detection works correctly with A:\Nodejs path
Include proper error handling
Features:
Basic Electron window with React app
Hot reload during development
Production build optimization

buildHistory.prompt:
Project Setup:
Use Tauri 2.x (latest version)
React 18 with TypeScript

Features Required:

Tauri command to get system info
Proper security configuration (CSP enabled)

Comprehensive README with troubleshooting
All necessary configuration files

Content Security Policy properly configured
Security meta tags in index.html
Modern dependencies (no outdated packages)
Proper TypeScript configuration
Build Verification:
Project should build successfully
Development mode should work
No missing files or broken references
Please create this Tauri v2.x project with all modern best practices and security measures in place."

buildHistory.description:
"Create a new Tauri + React project called 'PrimeTest' with the following specifications:
Modern Ta...
buildHistory.prompt:
"Create a new Tauri + React project called 'PrimeTest' with the following specifications:
Modern Tauri v2 Setup:
Use Tauri 2.x (NOT v1.5 - completely different config format)
React 18 with TypeScript
Modern Vite build system
Features Required:
Basic React app with a counter component
Tauri command to get system information (CPU, memory, OS)
File system operations (read/write text files)
Proper security configuration (CSP enabled)
Window management (minimize, maximize, close)
Project Structure:
Complete directory structure with icons folder
Proper .gitignore file for Tauri projects
Comprehensive README with setup and troubleshooting
All necessary configuration files (tauri.conf.json v2 format)
Security & Best Practices:
Content Security Policy properly configured (not null)
Security meta tags in index.html
Modern dependencies (latest React, TypeScript, Vite)
Proper TypeScript configuration
Tauri v2 Specific Requirements:
Use @tauri-apps/plugin-shell for shell operations
Configure plugins properly in Rust code
Use correct v2 API patterns
Proper Cargo.toml with modern features
Build Verification:
Project should build successfully on first try
Development mode should work without errors
No missing files or broken references
All dependencies should resolve correctly
